results.title: An honest look at ElevenLabs reviews: Is it the right AI voice for you ...
results.content:
A common complaint you'll see in user reviews is frustration with this pricing model. People often say that credits disappear much faster than they expect, especially when using the dubbing feature or higher-quality voices. Another big issue is that unused credits don't roll over. If you have a slow month, you just lose them, which makes it tough for people with inconsistent workloads to feel like they're getting their money's worth.

This is a very different approach from platforms like eesel AI, which offers straightforward pricing based on your overall usage. With eesel AI, you don't have to count every interaction or worry about a massive bill after a busy month.

## Key limitations [...] ### Billing and support concerns

A quick scroll through Trustpilot shows a pattern of complaints about billing problems, the confusing credit policy, and customer support that is slow or unhelpful. For an individual creator, that's annoying. For a business that relies on the platform for an important part of its operations, it's a serious risk.

## A great tool for creators, but not built for support teams

After digging through all the reviews and features, the takeaway is pretty clear. ElevenLabs is a fantastic, top-of-the-line tool for anyone who needs to generate realistic AI voices for content. Podcasters, YouTubers, audiobook producers, and developers who just need a best-in-class TTS engine will get a ton of value out of it. [...] ### AI dubbing and translation

The AI dubbing tool can translate your audio or video into 29 languages, all while trying to keep the original speaker's tone. It's a fantastic feature for creators trying to go global. That said, some users have hit snags. A common headache is that the dubbing tool can chew through character credits unexpectedly fast, leaving people with surprise bills and a lot of frustration.

### The AI agents platform

More recently, ElevenLabs stepped into the world of conversational AI with its Agents Platform, which is aimed at developers building voice-bots for places like call centers. It offers an excellent, low-latency voice, which is a must-have for any voice-based agent.

results.title: Why voice AI agents fail: A discussion with ElevenLabs - LinkedIn
results.content: that killed conversations in the real world. The tech is brilliant, but the experience was broken. This isn’t just a bug, it's a core architectural trade-off that forces a classic trilemma: Latency vs. Cost vs. Accuracy. Gemini's default "thinking" feature boosts accuracy but drives up latency and token costs. Developers must choose between the Native Audio model for stunning emotional realism (but weaker tool-use) and the Half-Cascade model for production-grade reliability (at the cost of vocal quality). The dream is a voice agent that's fast, smart, and affordable. Isn’t it? The path forward, IMO: Hyper-Personalization is Non-Negotiable The future isn't one perfect voice; it's giving users control to create their own. The next wave of agents will remember user preferences and maintain [...] Why are customers still annoyed with AI voice agents which are technically superior? Models from OpenAI, Gemini, and ElevenLabs are more capable than ever, yet customer frustration persists and voice AI model landscape is in a paradox. ChatGPT's Advance Voice mode was a remarkable technological achievement, offering fluid and natural human-AI conversation in real-time. But OpenAI had to rollback to Standard Voice as default mode due to the backlash regarding its accuracy. It proves users don't just care about specs; they care about connection and consistency. On the flipside, Google's Gemini Live API, with its sophisticated Affective Dialogue capabilities for a mindful conversation was plagued by random, 10-second latency spikes that killed conversations in the real world. The tech is [...] want to stitch together multiple AI vendors, they want end-to-end platforms that simplify the stack and reduce latency. - Demos often look impressive, but very few companies can scale real-time voice tech reliably in production environments. - AI voice agents that sound expressive aren't enough — turn-taking and accuracy are still bigger challenges. - Most companies ignore accessibility in AI, but modeling things like stuttering actually improves agent behavior. - Streaming speech and voice models will unlock more lifelike, responsive AI agents — and it’s coming fast. - Audio AI needs deep expertise beyond AI, including sound engineering and context-aware modeling of human speech. - There’s a growing trend of AI companies going beyond voice to control the full audio experience, including

results.title: My Disappointing Experience with Eleven Labs' AI | Theo Tabah ...
results.content:
I wrestled with 11 AI and lost. Lost bad. This product, while in beta, is not ready for primetime. I love Eleven Labs and think they're doing some revolutionary stuff, but a mixed bag of bugs, design opportunities, and AX (Agentic Experience) misses made for quite a hilarious, and frustrating few minutes with Elle. Some quick hits: 1. When muted, be absolute certain the AI is not listening. Immediate trust-breaker. Unfortunately Elle was listening to everything, even on mute. 2. Make pacing and cadence easy to change. Even within the same voice. This one was terribly slow and monotonous. 3. Latency is a killer. Silence breeds doubt. Fix this to help with a more fluid conversation like one would expect with another person. 4. Make swapping voices easy - some design speed bumps and [...] Jin Park 🧠

results.title: AI Voice Agents: ElevenLabs Review - by Lucia Franzese
results.content:
Model performance challenges: There seem to still be some issues around spoken numbers. If you’re expecting your AI Agent to whiz through many numbers, you will want to do extensive testing with the models.
 Scalability challenges: ElevenLabs built the concept of concurrency into their pricing. For the enterprise plan, you max out at 30 concurrent agents. So, for example, if you have 30 agents on the phone and one incremental call comes in, you won’t be able to answer it until the next agent is available. This may present scalability challenges, though more likely for a call center than a Sales team. [...] ##### Support Desk

The AI Agent will be no good unless you’ve “trained” them up by providing access to your knowledge base. This is one area where I think ElevenLabs is a bit lacking. You can upload a document that details your knowledge base, but we all know how quickly that will become outdated. Alternatively, while ElevenLabs offers some integrations, such as with ZenDesk and ServiceNow, it is missing key systems, such as Confluence and Hubspot’s Knowledge base. I expect they’re planning to incorporate other systems, but in the meantime, making sure that the AI Agent is sourcing accurate information will prove somewhat challenging.

##### Scheduling & Communication [...] Therefore, ElevenLabs may not be a good fit for organizations that:

 Need to run deep analytics
 Are unwilling to give prompt engineering real resourcing
 Have a complicated product with a frequently updated knowledge base
 Are very large and might require more than 30 concurrent AI agents

I would, however, recommend this product if:

 Your sales team has a high volume of leads that you’re either not getting to today or require several SDRs to reach them
 Your team has historically not called certain leads because the expected LTV was too low
 You’re a start up getting started and you can access the product for free with the ElevenLabs grant
 You’re in a highly-regulated industry that needs to carefully mind the data privacy side
